title: Differences between King Records and Gainax R2J DVDs
display_title: Differences between King Records and Gainax R2J DVDs
wikitext:
[]: Differences between King Records and Gainax R2J DVDs             Quote Post by svenge » Sun Aug 01, 2010 7:23 am Are there any differences in terms of content between the King Records and Gainax original (pre-remaster) releases of the Eva TV? Catalog numbers: King Records: KIBA-1 through KIBA-7 Gainax: GDVD-001 through GDVD-007 Any info would be appreciated.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Thu Aug 05, 2010 12:06 am That's it. I was planning on never logging in again, but 63 views and no replies angered me so much that I had to respond. I'm on the motherfucking case.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Thu Aug 05, 2010 2:36 am Thanks, I want to know this, too.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Mon Aug 09, 2010 5:50 pm Well, I got my KIBA-1~7 set in the mail today, and it solved the mystery. I played the first and last discs in my PC, and the disc labels were "GDVD1_KIBA1" and "GDVD007_KIBA7" respectively. I'm not surprised by the minor inconsistency of the disc titles since #1 was put out in mid-1997 and #7 wasn't released until early 1999. So, except for the different cover art, there's no real difference in content for the 7 TV discs. I don't have KIBA-105/6 or its GDVD-008/9 counterpart for the movies, so I can't confirm those two. I'd assume the same holds for them, though.

[]: The Second Impact Box Set             Quote Post by Alpine » Sat Apr 23, 2011 6:41 am Hi - I realise that this is waaay overdue, but I only joined this forum a short time ago - at least, that's my excuse. I actually own the GDVD Box Sets - called the "Second Impact Box Sets" No. 1 to 3. They were released well before any of the renewal took place, and they're different 'cause they have the original TV series - as they were broadcast - without any adjustments. When released for retail, anime episodes often get touched up a bit - not so much the animation, but in Eva's case the eyecatch titles were changed or shuffled around. As far as I'm aware, unless you sat down and recorded these episodes in 1995 when Eva was first broadcast, these DVDs are the only ones sold of the series, as they were shown on Japanese TV. Volume 3 also has the movies, and the 'Girlfriend of Steel' game for Windows. I can't comment on quality of video per se, but they should be similar. I've actually never played my box set, as I had Eva in another form). Hope this helps
